items.title: Apple drops commission rates in China without a fuss
items.content: Apple dropped its App Store commission rates to 25% in China, and commissions on auto-renewed subscriptions down to 12%.

items.title: Former Apple engineer raises $5M for a note-taking pendant that only records your voice
items.content: Taya is trying to address privacy concerns around note-taking wearables with its device that can be worn as a pendant and records only the user's voice.

items.title: US military contractor likely built iPhone hacking tools used by Russian spies in Ukraine
items.content: Google found a series of hacking tools they said were used by a Russian espionage group and a cybercriminal group in China. Sources from a U.S. government defense contractor s

items.title: Apple now makes 1 in 4 iPhones in India: Report
items.content: Apple reportedly produced 55 million units in India last year, a shift accelerated as Apple faced growing uncertainty in China.

items.title: The billion-dollar infrastructure deals powering the AI boom
items.content: Here's everything we know about the biggest AI infrastructure projects, including major spending from Meta, Oracle, Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI.

items.title: The White House wants AI companies to cover rate hikes. Most have already said they would.
items.content: Many hyperscalers have already made public commitments to cover electricity cost increases.

items.title: The public opposition to AI infrastructure is heating up
items.content: Public backlash over the data center boom is leading to a variety of draconian policies — including bans on new construction.

items.title: All the important news from the ongoing India AI Impact Summit
items.content: India is hosting a four-day AI Summit this week that will be attended by executives from major AI labs and Big Tech, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Nvidia, Microsoft, Google, an

items.title: California’s Billionaire Tax Battle
items.content: Wealthy residents of the state have put millions of dollars toward stopping a proposed 5 percent tax on their assets.

items.title: Justice Dept. Legal Threat Complicates Trump’s Pick for Fed Chair
items.content: An investigation of the Federal Reserve was thwarted on Friday, but a department appeal could reimpose an obstacle in Kevin Warsh’s path.

items.title: When Changing Your Address Leads to Losing Your Medicare Coverage
items.content: Using a post office box shouldn’t cost you your health insurance. But in one woman’s case, it sure seemed to.

items.title: America Depends Less on Oil Than Ever
items.content: Gasoline still drives household budgets, but energy efficiency and renewables have reduced the economy’s overall reliance on petroleum.
